label: Uneasy calm
summary: The world ticked up a point but stayed lodged in its uneasy-calm band as US-Iran fighting entered a second day even while mediators worked to pull both sides back. Overnight the United States struck roughly 90 military targets across Iran, hitting missile and drone stores, a northeastern railway bridge and port cities including Bandar Abbas and Chabahar; Iran's Revolutionary Guard retaliated against US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar and Jordan, firing ten ballistic missiles at Jordan's Azraq base. Iran's health ministry put the two-day toll at about 14 killed and 78 injured. Yet Pakistan and Qatar, who brokered June's Islamabad Memorandum, made fresh contacts urging both capitals back to talks, and Qatar's prime minister pressed Iran to honour the deal. Markets read the diplomacy as the stronger signal: the Nasdaq rose 1.3 percent to 26,207 and the S&P 500 gained 0.81 percent as Brent crude eased 2.1 percent to $76.39. The rest of the ledger stayed heavy. Israeli fire killed at least nine Palestinians in Gaza despite the ceasefire; the RSF's siege of El Obeid kept half a million trapped in darkness; Venezuela's twin-quake toll climbed toward 3,900 amid national mourning; and Ebola deaths in DR Congo passed 500 as Uganda tracked an isolated Marburg case. Super Typhoon Bavi drew a land warning for Taiwan, suspending ferries ahead of torrential rain before it tracks toward eastern China. Against that ran real lift: Eli Lilly's oral pill orforglipron beat oral semaglutide on weight and blood-sugar control in a head-to-head diabetes trial, and NATO's Ukraine backing and Patriot-production licence held. The headline settles at 41, buoyed by market calm and active mediation but capped by a second day of open Middle East fighting.

drivers.headline: The United States and Iran trade attacks for a second day, further straining their fragile ceasefire, as US forces strike roughly 90 military targets across Iran overnight including missile and drone stores, a northeastern railway bridge, bases in Bushehr province and the port cities of Bandar Abbas, Konarak and Chabahar, and Iran's Revolutionary Guard retaliates against US installations in Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar and Jordan, firing ten ballistic missiles at Jordan's Azraq air base, with Iran's health ministry putting the two-day toll at about 14 killed and 78 injured

Pakistan and Qatar, the two mediators who brokered June's Islamabad Memorandum, make fresh contacts with Washington and Tehran to halt further military strikes and bring both sides back to the negotiating table 'as per agreement,' with Qatar's prime minister urging Iran to commit to implementing the deal and to resolve disputes through diplomacy rather than force, in the first concerted diplomatic push since President Trump declared the truce 'over'

US stocks rise and oil prices ease as calm returns to financial markets worldwide, with the Nasdaq Composite gaining 1.30 percent to 26,206.89, the S&P 500 climbing 0.81 percent to 7,543.64 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average adding 139 points to 52,487.41, led by a 2.5 percent jump in semiconductors, while Brent crude falls 2.1 percent to $76.39 a barrel as investors set aside the flare-up in US-Iran hostilities and focus on AI prospects ahead of SK Hynix's US trading debut

Israeli air attacks and gunfire kill at least nine Palestinians in Gaza in 24 hours, including two children, despite the ceasefire in effect since last October, with an air raid killing one person near a Gaza City school, an attack on a tent sheltering displaced people in al-Mawasi killing at least four including a 10-year-old, and a food-aid driver for World Central Kitchen among the dead, as rights monitors count more than 1,090 Palestinians killed during the nine-month 'ceasefire'

The Rapid Support Forces sustain their siege of El Obeid, capital of Sudan's North Kordofan state, leaving roughly half a million civilians trapped in a city without electricity for nearly a month, as the UN human rights office documents ten consecutive days of drone strikes killing at least 50 civilians and rights groups warn the city could become the next El-Fasher-style atrocity should a full ground assault begin

Venezuela remains in national mourning as the death toll from its June 24 twin earthquakes climbs toward 3,900, with authorities reporting nearly 18,000 people displaced and forensic teams still recovering and identifying bodies from the rubble more than two weeks after back-to-back magnitude-7.2 and 7.5 quakes struck seconds apart, as survivors hold candlelit vigils in Caracas and Maracaibo and hopes of finding further survivors fade

The Ebola death toll in the Democratic Republic of the Congo surpasses 500 with more than 1,560 confirmed cases in an outbreak driven by the Bundibugyo virus, for which there is no approved vaccine or specific treatment, as Uganda tracks an isolated Marburg virus case in a child who died in Kyegegwa district, straining regional health systems fighting two viral haemorrhagic fevers at once in what the WHO has declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern

Taiwan's Central Weather Administration issues a land warning for Super Typhoon Bavi, forecasting the storm's greatest impact from Friday evening through Saturday with maximum sustained winds of 162 kph, gusts to 198 kph and up to 900mm of rain in mountainous areas, as ferries to outlying islands and China are suspended and Bavi, set to be the largest storm by size to strike Taiwan since 1987, tracks northwest toward eastern China and the Shanghai region

French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for the presidency in 2027 despite a Paris appeals court convicting her of embezzlement, fining her 100,000 euros and ordering her to wear an electronic monitor for a year, with Le Pen vowing to appeal to France's highest court, a step she says will suspend the monitoring order and let her campaign without an ankle bracelet, in a ruling the court framed with the voter's freedom of choice in mind

New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says his country is actively considering joining the Ocean of Peace Alliance, the mutual-defence pact Australia signed with Fiji, calling engagement 'logical' given enduring military ties with Canberra and Suva, as Wellington and Canberra deepen Pacific security cooperation amid concern over external military competition in the region following China's recent long-range missile test into the Pacific

Eli Lilly's once-daily oral pill orforglipron delivers greater weight loss and blood-sugar control than oral semaglutide in a head-to-head Phase 3 type 2 diabetes trial published in The Lancet, offering a needle-free GLP-1 option that requires no refrigeration, is cheaper to manufacture and could expand access globally, months after the drug won FDA approval for weight management

President Trump wraps the NATO summit in Ankara on a positive note after meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, granting Ukraine a licence to produce Patriot air-defence systems domestically as the alliance closes with a 70-billion-euro assistance pledge for Ukraine's fight against Russia, giving Kyiv a path to the interceptors it says it urgently needs after Russia's deadly missile and drone barrage on the capital days earlier

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (2026 Iran war / 2026 Iran war ceasefire / 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis / Typhoon Bavi 2026 / 2026 Venezuela earthquakes / 2026 Ebola epidemic / Gaza war / 2027 French presidential election)
